expectations.text: Recommends a separate BillingDbContext owned by the Billing module (does NOT recommend sharing/extending the host's existing DbContext)
expectations.evidence: Each module in a modular monolith owns its own DbContext... Sharing one DbContext across modules looks tempting but defeats the entire point of modularity; section 'Why per-module DbContext (don't share one across modules)'.

expectations.text: Specifies a unique EF Core migrations history table for this module — explicitly something like __EFMigrationsHistory_Billing — and explains why (avoid EF treating other modules' migrations as missing)
expectations.evidence: MigrationsHistoryTable("__EFMigrationsHistory_Billing"). This is the line that prevents cross-module conflicts. If you leave the default __EFMigrationsHistory, EF sees migrations rows from your two other modules and tries to re-apply them (or fails because the rows don't match Billing's migration ids).

expectations.text: Includes an IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<BillingDbContext> implementation alongside the DbContext, explaining it's needed for `dotnet ef migrations add` (or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: BillingDbContextFactory : IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<BillingDbContext>... EF Core tooling (dotnet ef migrations add, Update-Database) cannot boot the full ABP host, so each module ships its own design-time factory.

expectations.text: Includes a per-module schema migrator: an IBillingDbSchemaMigrator interface and an EntityFrameworkCoreBillingDbSchemaMigrator (or equivalently named) implementation that runs migrations on the module's DbContext
expectations.evidence: public interface IBillingDbSchemaMigrator { Task MigrateAsync(); } and class EntityFrameworkCoreBillingDbSchemaMigrator : IBillingDbSchemaMigrator, ITransientDependency calling ctx.Database.MigrateAsync().

expectations.text: Wires the DbContext with the PostgreSQL provider (UseNpgsql) — i.e. the answer is template-aware about Postgres, not a generic SQL Server example
